user_query:
I’m preparing a short, data-backed health analysis from the factory floor and need your help to turn my notes into something shareable. Please use the provided inputs and produce all three deliverables below.

Inputs:
- input/worker_health_1846_1850.csv — factory workforce and cases by sector (Textiles, Coal Mining, Ironworks) for 1846–1850
- input/pamphlet_draft.md — a pamphlet draft with a placeholder section to replace
- input/mentor_contact.json — my mentor’s contact details for the email greeting

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Compose a health trends report at outputs/health_trends_report.md that:
   - Uses input/worker_health_1846_1850.csv to compute, for each year–sector row, the respiratory illness rate and injury rate per 1,000 workers (clearly show the raw counts and computed rates).
   - Includes an Overview section with exactly these three bullet points, each with explicit start and end numbers derived from the data:
     • Coal Mining respiratory illness rate change from 1846 to 1850 (per 1,000)
     • Textiles injury rate change from 1846 to 1850 (per 1,000)
     • Total workforce size change across all sectors from 1846 to 1850 (raw counts)
   - Includes a Methods section (brief: data source path, how rates were calculated, and any rounding you applied).
   - Includes a Statistics section that lists every year–sector with: year, sector, workers, respiratory_cases, injury_cases, respiratory_rate_per_1000, injury_rate_per_1000. Also provide grand totals across 1846–1850 for workers, respiratory_cases, injury_cases, plus the corresponding overall rates per 1,000.
   - Includes a simple quality control footer with:
     • Rows processed: 15 (should match the CSV data rows)
     • Grand totals echo (workers, respiratory_cases, injury_cases) taken from your calculations above

2) Edit the pamphlet by rewriting the placeholder section and save it as outputs/pamphlet_updated.md:
   - Open input/pamphlet_draft.md and replace the exact token [TO_FILL_HEALTH_TRENDS] with a 100–150 word, plain-language summary for general readers that states the same three trends listed in the report’s Overview (use the computed numbers and specify “per 1,000” when referring to rates). Do not change any other lines or headings.

3) Draft an email at outputs/email_to_mentor.txt:
   - Subject: Industrial Health Trends Report (1846–1850) — Request for Feedback
   - Greeting line must use input/mentor_contact.json’s preferred_salutation and name fields (e.g., “Dear Mrs. Ada Whitfield,”).
   - Body: 1–2 short paragraphs that summarize the purpose of the analysis and reference at least one exact numeric rate you computed in the report (e.g., the Coal Mining respiratory illness rate change), and mention both attachments by path: outputs/health_trends_report.md and outputs/pamphlet_updated.md.
   - Close with a polite sign-off (e.g., “Sincerely, Your apprentice”).

Notes:
- Base all calculations strictly on input/worker_health_1846_1850.csv; do not add or infer rows.
- Keep file names and paths exactly as specified.
- All three operations are required: you must compute and compose the report, edit/rewrite the pamphlet section, and draft the email.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
